title: aethusa cynapium subsp. agrestis
intro: Aethusa cynapium subsp. agrestis, also known as wild hemlock, is a subspecies of the hemlock plant that grows in various parts of the world, particularly in Europe and Asia. This plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to stimulate digestion and relieve flatulence, as well as its potential to act as a diuretic. The health benefits of aethusa cynapium subsp. agrestis include its potential to reduce inflammation and improve circulation, although it is essential to note that excessive consumption can be toxic. Herbal preparations of wild hemlock often involve the use of its roots or seeds in teas, tinctures, or infusions, although it is crucial to consult with a medical professional before using this plant for medicinal purposes.
